retrieved.title: Health during your studies
retrieved.text:
Just five minutes of exercise in green spaces reduce stress and negative moods.

Strengthen your everyday student life with small, conscious decisions about nutrition.

Eating a small handful of nuts every day can have a positive effect on heart health.

Vegetarian and vegan dishes are available daily at two locations at the University of Vienna.

- [Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-oskar-morgenstern-platz-1/)
- [Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-djerassiplatz-1-ubb/)

[From master´s thesis to optimised nutrition](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studierende/ernaehrung-im-studium/)

In this post, student Denny explains how to eat healthier in everyday student life.

Well-being starts in the mind. Strategies to strengthen resilience and focus on studying can help.

[Tips and tricks for dealing with stressful periods of study can be found on the blog.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/facing-challenges-wie-kann-ich-meine-resilienz-staerken/)

[Decisiveness, control over your own reactions and more.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/resilienz-im-studium-strategien-fuer-mehr-handlungskompetenz/)

[Support through psychological counselling and psychotherapeutic methods.](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/)

retrieved.title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
retrieved.text:
"Team Barrierefrei" offers counselling and information related to studying and student life for students with a disability, impairment or disease.

- [Studying with disabilities](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [University of Vienna Accessbile Studies](/en/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

retrieved.title: Web services
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)

- [Moodle](#c242239)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

heading_path: Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna
Grüne Schule at the Botanical Garden
retrieved.text:
Who knows the trick of the lotus blossom? What does a living fossil look like? Where does Vienna's biggest bamboo grove grow?

The Botanical Garden of the University of Vienna offers school classes a unique opportunity to experience nature and explore plants in the middle of the city.

The aim of the project Grüne Schule (Green School) is to give pupils an understanding of the fascinating world of plants. Guided tours and workshops stimulate the interest in researching and exploring nature. Expertise, stunning details and inspiring histories as well as the didactic mediation take centre stage - an exciting and adecuate provision of knowledge for our young generation.

[Click here to know more](http://grueneschule.univie.ac.at) (in German)

© Universität Wien / derknopfdruecker.com

heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
retrieved.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

retrieved.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
retrieved.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: Services for graduates
retrieved.text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: Health during your studies
reranked.text:
Just five minutes of exercise in green spaces reduce stress and negative moods.

Strengthen your everyday student life with small, conscious decisions about nutrition.

Eating a small handful of nuts every day can have a positive effect on heart health.

Vegetarian and vegan dishes are available daily at two locations at the University of Vienna.

- [Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-oskar-morgenstern-platz-1/)
- [Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-djerassiplatz-1-ubb/)

[From master´s thesis to optimised nutrition](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studierende/ernaehrung-im-studium/)

In this post, student Denny explains how to eat healthier in everyday student life.

Well-being starts in the mind. Strategies to strengthen resilience and focus on studying can help.

[Tips and tricks for dealing with stressful periods of study can be found on the blog.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/facing-challenges-wie-kann-ich-meine-resilienz-staerken/)

[Decisiveness, control over your own reactions and more.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/resilienz-im-studium-strategien-fuer-mehr-handlungskompetenz/)

[Support through psychological counselling and psychotherapeutic methods.](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/)

reranked.title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
reranked.text:
"Team Barrierefrei" offers counselling and information related to studying and student life for students with a disability, impairment or disease.

- [Studying with disabilities](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [University of Vienna Accessbile Studies](/en/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

heading_path: Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna
Grüne Schule at the Botanical Garden
reranked.text:
Who knows the trick of the lotus blossom? What does a living fossil look like? Where does Vienna's biggest bamboo grove grow?

The Botanical Garden of the University of Vienna offers school classes a unique opportunity to experience nature and explore plants in the middle of the city.

The aim of the project Grüne Schule (Green School) is to give pupils an understanding of the fascinating world of plants. Guided tours and workshops stimulate the interest in researching and exploring nature. Expertise, stunning details and inspiring histories as well as the didactic mediation take centre stage - an exciting and adecuate provision of knowledge for our young generation.

[Click here to know more](http://grueneschule.univie.ac.at) (in German)

heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
reranked.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

reranked.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
reranked.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: Services for graduates
reranked.text:
**Returning** You can participate in a number of different events held by the University of Vienna in order to stay in touch with academia. Under the motto “academia at the heart of the city”, the University of Vienna offers guided tours, panel discussions, exhibitions and many other events to provide insight into the different research areas at the University.

- [Event calendar of the University of Vienna](https://kalender.univie.ac.at/) (in German)
- [Events at the Campus of the University of Vienna](https://campus.univie.ac.at/en/20-years-of-university-of-vienna-campus/)

#### Alumni Newsletter

Once a month, you will receive the latest information from your Alma Mater and the Alumni Association - including event invitations and special offers for graduates. Let's stay in touch!

[Subscribe to the alumni newsletter](https://alumni.ac.at/portal/getinvolved/informiertbleiben) (in German).

You can find even more alumni activities on LinkedIn - [network with us](https://www.linkedin.com/company/alumni-uniwien/)!

#### univie magazine

The University of Vienna’s alumni magazine univie is published three times a year and provides information on current topics from the world of academia and practice.

- [univie — the University of Vienna’s alumni magazine](https://www.alumni.ac.at/univie-magazin) (in German)
- [Subscribe to univie](https://www.alumni.ac.at/portal/univiemagazin/abo/aboformular) (in German)

#### Rise magazine

Rise is the career magazine by Uniport. It is published once a semester and offers insight into different professions as well as advice on building your own career.

- [Rise — Uniport’s career magazine](https://www.uniport.at/fuer-arbeitgeber/portfolio/karrieremagazin-rise/) (in German)

2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

- Continuing education: On the website of the [Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.univie.ac.at/en/) you will find information on the requirements for admission to continuing education courses, certification courses and a lot more.

selected.title: Health during your studies
selected.text:
Just five minutes of exercise in green spaces reduce stress and negative moods.

Strengthen your everyday student life with small, conscious decisions about nutrition.

Eating a small handful of nuts every day can have a positive effect on heart health.

Vegetarian and vegan dishes are available daily at two locations at the University of Vienna.

- [Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-oskar-morgenstern-platz-1/)
- [Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/eating-and-drinking-at-djerassiplatz-1-ubb/)

[From master´s thesis to optimised nutrition](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studierende/ernaehrung-im-studium/)

In this post, student Denny explains how to eat healthier in everyday student life.

Well-being starts in the mind. Strategies to strengthen resilience and focus on studying can help.

[Tips and tricks for dealing with stressful periods of study can be found on the blog.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/facing-challenges-wie-kann-ich-meine-resilienz-staerken/)

[Decisiveness, control over your own reactions and more.](https://blog.univie.ac.at/studium/resilienz-im-studium-strategien-fuer-mehr-handlungskompetenz/)

[Support through psychological counselling and psychotherapeutic methods.](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/)

selected.title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
selected.text:
"Team Barrierefrei" offers counselling and information related to studying and student life for students with a disability, impairment or disease.

- [Studying with disabilities](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [University of Vienna Accessbile Studies](/en/)

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

The cafeterias offer reasonable meals. Everyone is allowed to eat there, a permit or special ID is not needed. To get meals even cheaper you can obtain the so-called "Mensenpickerl" at the Austrian National Union of Students (Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft - ÖH).

Apart from the cafeterias, students like to go to the numerous restaurants and cafés located around the main building where meals and snacks are relatively cheap.

- [Cafeterias](http://www.mensen.at/) - good food for a reasonable price
- [ÖH - Mensenpickerl](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/page/canteen-sticker)- eat even cheaper

As long as you are studying at the University of Vienna you have to pay the fees according to your citizenship (in some cases only the Students' Union fee, in some cases more) every semester in time. The tuition fee depends on your citzenship and, for EU/EEA citizens, also on the duration of your studies.

- [Students' Union fee/tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

There are several types of financial support available for students. Obtain information about allowances/grants for studying abroad in your home country. Student discounts can make life easier - at least in financial terms.

selected.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
selected.text: Image 1 of 1 The main building of the University of Vienna houses several institutes, such as the Institute of History, as well as central service facilities, such as the University Library. © University of Vienna / Alex Schuppich The arcade courtyard can be found inside the main building: Here you can learn in good weather or just enjoy the sun. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair The largest lecture hall in the main building of the University of Vienna is the Audimax, which offers more than 750 seats in the hall and on the balcony. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl You can also learn and work well in other locations of the University Library - the Departmental Libraries - for example in the Education, Linguistics and Comparative Literature Library in Sensengasse 3a. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair A location of the University of Vienna near the main building is the NIG - the New Institute Building. Many University Departments are located here like the Department of Cultural and Social Anthropology or of Philosophy. © University of Vienna / Peter Wienerroither Another location of the University of Vienna is the Campus. Here you will find, for example, the Department of Art History or of Romance Studies. With its many green courtyards, the Campus offers space for rest and relaxation in the middle of the city.

selected.title: Our offers and projects
selected.text:
On this page you will find current offers and projects from "School meets Uni" as well as from other institutions of the University of Vienna, which are aimed specifically at pupils, teachers and school classes as well as at those students, who have recently started their studies at the University of Vienna:

- [Dr. Hans Riegel prizes](#c539634)
- [School meets Uni - Offers for school classes](#c319792)
- [Youth Science Competition of the University of Vienna](/en/degree-programmes/we-cooperate-with/#c274336)
- [Further offers for pupils and school classes at the University of Vienna](#c409284)

© Universität Wien/ Martin Sowa

As part of the project "School meets Uni", we offer school classes the opportunity to visit the University of Vienna and "get a taste of university life". In one-hour, free of cost information lectures, we provide a general overview of the differences between school and university, our studies and the admission procedure at the University of Vienna.

Are you interested in a school lecture for your class? Just send us an email: schule-trifft-uni@univie.ac.at.
